2606.12411.title: Context-Driven Incremental Compression for Multi-Turn Dialogue Generation
2606.12411.paper_summary: Modern conversational agents condition on an ever-growing dialogue history at each turn, incurring redundant attention and encoding costs that grow with conversation length. Naive truncation or summarization degrades fidelity, while existing context compressors lack cross-turn memory sharing or revision, causing information loss and compounding errors in long dialogues. We revisit the context compression under conversational dynamics and empirically present its fragility. To improve both efficiency and robustness, we introduce Context-Driven Incremental Compression (C-DIC), which treats a conversation as interleaved contextual threads and stores revisable per-thread compression states in a single, compact dialogue memory. At each turn, a lightweight retrieve, revise, and write-back loop shares information across turns and updates stale memories, stabilizing long-horizon behavior. In addition, we adapt truncated backpropagation-through-time (TBPTT) to our multi-turn setting, learning cross-turn dependencies without full-history backpropagation. Extensive experiments on long-form dialogue benchmarks demonstrate superior performance and efficiency of C-DIC; notably, C-DIC shows stable inference latency and perplexity over hundreds of dialogue turns, supporting a scalable path to high-quality dialogue modeling.
2606.12411.paper_summary_zh: 現代對話代理在每次交互中都依賴於不斷增長的對話歷史，這導致隨著對話長度的增加而產生冗餘的注意力和編碼成本。天真的截斷或摘要會降低真實性，而現有的上下文壓縮器缺乏跨輪次的記憶共享或修訂，導致信息損失並在長對話中加劇錯誤。我們重新審視了對話動態下的上下文壓縮，並實證地展示了其脆弱性。為了提高效率和穩健性，我們引入了上下文驅動的增量壓縮（C-DIC），將對話視為交錯的上下文線索，並在單一的緊湊對話記憶中存儲可修訂的每條線索壓縮狀態。在每次交互中，輕量級的檢索、修訂和寫回循環在輪次之間共享信息並更新陳舊的記憶，穩定長期行為。此外，我們將截斷的時間反向傳播（TBPTT）適應於我們的多輪設置，學習跨輪次的依賴關係，而無需全歷史反向傳播。在長篇對話基準上的廣泛實驗顯示了C-DIC的優越性能和效率；值得注意的是，C-DIC在數百輪對話中顯示出穩定的推理延遲和困惑度，支持高質量對話建模的可擴展路徑。
2606.12411.author: Yeongseo Jung et.al.
2606.12411.authors: Yeongseo Jung, Jaehyeok Kim, Eunseo Jung, Jiachuan Wang, Yongqi Zhang, Ka Chun Cheung, Simon See, Lei Chen
